description: GET /contexts/search response containing both 'matches' (the v0.1.0 wire field) AND 'results' (a forbidden synonym some implementations have shipped). The search-response schema is CLOSED (additionalProperties: false; see acdp-search-response.schema.json line 24 and the openness map in RFC-ACDP-0007 §3.3.1). Consumers MUST reject this response as schema_violation. Pairs with vis-003 which addresses the registry-side rule (only emit 'matches', never 'results').

expected.rationale: The closed search-response schema does not permit 'results'. Even though the field looks innocuous, accepting it would let two parallel response shapes coexist in deployed code and silently mask the field-name bug vis-003 catches.
expected.registry_outcome: registry MUST NOT emit this shape; doing so is a registry conformance failure
